varieties.name: Standard Wild Type (Mediterranean Origin)
varieties.origin: Southern Europe, Mediterranean Basin, Ancient cultivation (2000+ years)
varieties.traits: 90-200cm tall, purple-violet flowers June-August (Southern Hemisphere: Dec-Feb), white-veined leaves
varieties.yield_notes: 550-1680 kg/ha seeds, highly variable based on conditions
varieties.availability: Eden Seeds (QLD), Herbalistics (Australia), Boondie Seeds (Australia), All Rare Herbs (Australia)

varieties.name: 'Royston' (German Selection)
varieties.origin: Germany, modern breeding selection
varieties.traits: Selected for high silymarin content, uniform growth habit
varieties.yield_notes: Improved seed yield compared to wild populations
varieties.availability: Specialty seed suppliers, may require import from Germany

varieties.name: 'Silma' (Polish Cultivar)
varieties.origin: Poland, registered 1990s
varieties.traits: Bred for commercial cultivation, improved uniformity
varieties.yield_notes: Commercial-grade yields, over 2000 ha cultivated in Poland

varieties.name: 'Silyb' (Czech Cultivar)
varieties.origin: Czech Republic, registered 1988, Plant Variety Rights protected
varieties.traits: Developed for seed oil content and fatty acid composition

varieties.name: 'Budakalaszi Szibilla' (Hungarian Selection)
varieties.origin: Hungary, developed for Central European conditions
varieties.traits: Selected for medicinal quality, consistent flowering
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate to good yields, reliable producer

varieties.name: Queensland Coastal Ecotype
varieties.origin: Naturalised throughout coastal and sub-coastal Queensland
varieties.traits: Adapted to subtropical humidity, tolerates summer rainfall
varieties.yield_notes: High productivity in Brisbane climate, prolific self-seeding
varieties.availability: Wild-collected from non-protected areas, Australian seed banks

varieties.name: New South Wales Ecotype
varieties.origin: Naturalised throughout NSW, very common in coastal and sub-coastal regions
varieties.traits: 90-180cm tall, adapted to Australian conditions, robust growth
varieties.yield_notes: Prolific seed production, 6000+ seeds per plant
varieties.availability: Common in wild, seed saving from established plants

varieties.name: Victoria Ecotype
varieties.origin: Common in southern Victoria, adapted to temperate-Mediterranean
varieties.traits: Selected for cooler conditions but heat-tolerant

varieties.name: California Ecotype (USA)
varieties.origin: Naturalised throughout California, Mediterranean-type climate
varieties.traits: Drought adapted, heat tolerant, invasive in California
varieties.availability: California wild seed, may require import permits

varieties.name: New Zealand Ecotype
varieties.origin: Widely naturalised in New Zealand, low genetic variability
varieties.traits: Cool-season adapted but tolerates warm conditions
varieties.availability: New Zealand seed companies, breeding programs

varieties.name: Spanish/Mediterranean Ecotype
varieties.origin: Native range: Spain, Portugal, Italy, Mediterranean islands
varieties.traits: True wild type, maximum genetic diversity, heat and drought adapted
varieties.availability: European seed companies, heirloom seed banks

varieties.name: Standard Wild Type (Mediterranean Origin)
varieties.origin: Southern Europe, Mediterranean Basin
varieties.traits: 90-200cm tall, purple flowers, white-veined leaves
varieties.availability: Indian seed suppliers, Nurserylive.com, imports from international suppliers

varieties.name: 'Argintiu' (Moldovan Cultivar)
varieties.origin: Republic of Moldova
varieties.traits: Simultaneous seed maturation in flower heads - major breeding achievement
varieties.yield_notes: Improved commercial yields due to uniform maturation

varieties.name: 'Fortificator' (Moldovan Cultivar)
varieties.origin: Republic of Moldova, companion variety to Argintiu
varieties.traits: Selected for vigor and disease resistance

varieties.name: 'De Prahova' (Romanian Cultivar)
varieties.origin: Romania, local population selection
varieties.traits: Adapted to Romanian continental climate

varieties.name: Pakistani Ecotypes (Multiple Regions)
varieties.origin: Wild populations throughout Pakistan: Faisalabad, Gujranwala, Quetta, Kallar Kahar
varieties.traits: Diverse ecotypes from different climatic zones: cold semi-arid (Quetta), semi-arid (Faisalabad, Kallar Kahar), hot semi-arid (Gujranwala)
varieties.yield_notes: Adapted to different stress conditions, salinity tolerant
varieties.availability: Pakistani agricultural universities, research institutions (Quaid-i-Azam University)

varieties.name: North Indian Ecotypes (UP, Punjab, Haryana)
varieties.origin: Cultivated and naturalised in Uttar Pradesh, Punjab, Haryana, Rajasthan
varieties.traits: Adapted to dry Indian climates, heat tolerant
varieties.yield_notes: Contract farming schemes in place, growing demand
varieties.availability: Indian agricultural suppliers, contract farming companies, limited in Kerala

varieties.name: Tamil Nadu Ecotype
varieties.origin: Tamil Nadu cultivation areas
varieties.traits: Adapted to South Indian heat, some humidity tolerance

varieties.name: Iranian Ecotypes (Multiple Regions)
varieties.origin: Native to parts of Iran: Ghamsar-Barzak, Tandoureh, Golestan, Parvand, Sefid Kuhe, Karkheh
varieties.traits: Highly diverse ecotypes from different climatic zones, from Mediterranean to dry/saline regions
varieties.availability: Iranian research institutions, seed banks, may require import permits

varieties.name: 'Khoreslo Babak Castle' (Iranian Cultivar)
varieties.origin: Iran, named variety
varieties.traits: Selected for arid Iranian conditions

varieties.name: Egyptian Ecotypes (North, Middle, South)
varieties.origin: Wild populations along canal banks, roadsides throughout Egypt
varieties.traits: Adapted to Nile ecosystem, grows in varied Egyptian microclimates

varieties.name: Ethiopian Ecotype
varieties.origin: Southern limit of native African range
varieties.traits: Adapted to Ethiopian highlands and lowlands

varieties.name: Greek Ecotype
varieties.origin: Native to Greece, ancient cultivation history
varieties.traits: Wild Mediterranean type, high genetic diversity

varieties.name: Italian Ecotypes (Sardinian Populations)
varieties.origin: Native to Italy, strong Sardinian populations studied
varieties.traits: Two distinct chemotypes, different silymarin profiles
varieties.availability: Italian research institutions, Sardinian seed collectors

varieties.name: French Ecotype
varieties.origin: Native to southern France, Mediterranean regions
varieties.traits: Traditional French herbal medicine populations
varieties.availability: French heirloom seed companies, herbal suppliers

varieties.name: Bulgarian Ecotype
varieties.origin: Naturalised in Bulgaria, commercial cultivation
varieties.traits: Adapted to continental climate, cold winters, hot summers

varieties.name: Turkish Ecotype
varieties.origin: Native to Türkiye (Anatolia), within natural range
varieties.traits: Ancient populations, part of original gene pool

varieties.name: Afghan Ecotype
varieties.origin: Native populations in Afghanistan
varieties.traits: Adapted to harsh, dry conditions, high altitude tolerance

varieties.name: West Himalayan Ecotype
varieties.origin: Native range extends to West Himalaya
varieties.traits: High altitude adaptation, cold tolerant

varieties.name: Nepal Ecotype
varieties.origin: Reported in Nepal within native range
varieties.traits: Mountain/hill adaptation

varieties.name: Korean Peninsula Ecotypes
varieties.origin: South Korea naturalised populations, North Korea reported
varieties.traits: Six inbred lines developed in South Korea from naturalised populations: M01 (Canada origin), M02 (Germany), M03 (North Korea), M04 (Moldova), M05-M06 (South Korea)
varieties.availability: South Korean research institutions, breeding programs

varieties.name: Chinese Ecotype
varieties.origin: Naturalised in parts of China
varieties.traits: Adapted to Chinese growing conditions, limited documentation
